questions.question: In an isothermal process, 33g of N₂ gas is used at a temperature of 212°C. If the volume increases by 16%, what is the work done by the gas?
The volume of air at a certain temperature and 10⁵ Pa pressure is 2m³. It is compressed isothermally to a pressure of 2 × 10⁵ Pa. What is the amount of heat released?
The total change in entropy in a Carnot cycle is-
options: ধনাত্মক ও ঋণাত্মক উভয়ই হতে পারে (can be both positive and negative)

questions.question: The temperature of the air inside a tire is 127°C and the pressure is 2 atm. If the tire suddenly bursts, what will be the final temperature?
What is the amount of heat absorbed/released in the cyclic process shown in the figure above?
A 67.2 L capacity cylinder contains He gas at STP. What is the amount of heat required to increase the temperature of the gas by 20°C?
Which of the following relationships between the efficiency (η) of a heat engine and the coefficient of performance (COP) of a refrigerator operating between the same temperatures is correct?
For an ideal gas, the value of Cₚ/Cᵥ is 1.667. What is the degree of freedom of the gas molecules?

Two sample gases A and B are initially in the same state. Sample A undergoes an adiabatic expansion and sample B undergoes an isothermal expansion. The final volume is the same for both processes. If the final pressures of A and B are Pₐ and Pₑ respectively, which of the following is correct?
A Carnot engine operating between -27°C and 127°C has a power of 250W. If it absorbs 259.74 J of heat per cycle, how many cycles does the engine complete per second?
What is the slope of the P-V graph in an isothermal process?
What is the change in entropy when 5 kg of water at 100°C is converted to steam?
A Carnot engine operates between 27°C and 180°C. The engine absorbs 8.6 × 10⁴J of heat from the heat source. What is the amount of work done by the engine?
What is the value of the molar specific heat of O₂ at constant volume?

3 mol of a gas with molar specific heat Cᵥ₁ and 2 mol of a gas with molar specific heat Cᵥ₂ are mixed together. If the temperature of 4 mol of the gas mixture is increased by 2°C, and the change in internal energy is 7 times the value of the molar specific heat of the first gas, which of the following relationships between the molar specific heats is correct?

250 mL of water from a glass is poured into a container filled with 1.25 L of water. If the temperatures of the water in the glass and the container are 80°C and 20°C respectively, what will be the temperature of the water after mixing?
A waterfall is 900 meters high. If it is assumed that half of the kinetic energy of the falling water is converted into heat, what will be the increase in temperature?
The temperature of an open room of volume 30 m³ increases from 17°C to 27°C due to the sun's heat. However, the air pressure remains 10⁵ Pa. How many air molecules will leave the room?
The coefficient of performance of a refrigerator is 4.6. If the amount of heat removed is 250 J, what is the work done?
In an isothermal process, 33g of N₂ gas is used at a temperature of 212°C. If the volume increases by 16%, what is the work done by the gas?
In an adiabatic process, 1.8 L of gas kept at 0.83 atm pressure is changed to a pressure and volume of 544.16 mm Hg and 2L. What is the work done by the gas? [γ = 1.4]
In a cyclic process, the internal energy of the gas-
A cylinder contains 0.001 m³ of gas at 300K temperature and 10⁶ Pa pressure. The gas is expanded adiabatically. The expansion ratio is 1:2. In this case, what is the work done? [γ = 1.4]
An ideal gas is taken through the path ABCA as shown in the figure above. If 2.4 cal of heat is released from the system, what is the value of the mechanical equivalent of heat?
A gas mixture contains 3 mol of O₂ and 5 mol of Ar gas at temperature T'. What is the total internal energy of the system?

If the internal temperature of a refrigerator is -3°C and the ambient temperature is 27°C, what amount of heat will be released to the environment per second? [1 × 10⁻³ unit of electrical energy is supplied to the refrigerator per hour.]
If the molar specific heat of a gas at constant pressure is 7.92 cal K⁻¹mol⁻¹, what is the degree of freedom of the gas?

Two sample gases A and B are initially in the same state. Gas A is expanded adiabatically and gas B is expanded isothermally. The final volume is the same for both processes. If the final temperatures of A and B are Tₐ and Tₑ, which of the following is correct?
A Carnot engine operating between 10°C and 333°C rejects 890 J of heat per cycle. If the engine completes one cycle in 2.5 s, what is the power of the engine?
What is the slope of the P-V graph in an adiabatic process?
What is the change in entropy to raise the temperature of 1 L of water from 20°C to 40°C?
In an adiabatic process, if the pressure of a diatomic gas is increased by 0.5%, the volume of the gas will decrease by-
What is the value of Cₚ for NH₃ gas?

3 mol of a gas with molar specific heat Cₚ₁ and 5 mol of a gas with molar specific heat Cₚ₂ are mixed together. If the temperature of 4 mol of the gas mixture is increased by 1°C, and the change in internal energy is equal to the molar specific heat of the second gas, which of the following is correct?

A student wants to cool 250 grams of Diet 7-Up, initially at 27°C, by adding ice at -18°C. How much ice must he add so that all the ice melts and the final temperature is 0°C? [Assume the container does not absorb any heat, the specific heat of 7-Up is 4186 Jkg⁻¹°C⁻¹, the latent heat of fusion of ice is 3.34 × 10⁵ Jkg⁻¹, and the specific heat of ice is 2000 Jkg⁻¹°C⁻¹]
What is the difference in entropy between 1g of water at 100°C and 1g of steam at 100°C under 1 atmospheric pressure? [Latent heat of vaporization of water at 100°C = 540 cal/gm]

The temperature of an open room of volume 30 m³ increases from 17°C to 22°C due to the sun's heat. However, the air pressure remains 10⁵ Pa. If the number of air molecules in the room before and after heating are n₁ and n₂ respectively, what is the value of n₁ - n₂?
The K of a refrigerator is 4.6. If 300 J of heat is removed from the cold chamber per cycle, what is the work done per cycle?
A certain amount of gas at 17°C is suddenly compressed so that the pressure of the gas becomes 4 times the initial pressure. If γ = 1.5, find the increase in temperature.
Find the change in entropy to raise the temperature of 12 kg of water from 15°C to 100°C. [Specific heat of water = 4200 Jkg⁻¹K⁻¹]
A refrigerator absorbs 3200 J of heat from a reservoir at -50°C and rejects 5500 J of heat. Find the coefficient of performance of the refrigerator. Find the temperature of the reservoir.

2 mol of an ideal gas is used in the ABCA cycle. The temperatures at points A and B are 300 K and 500 K respectively. The change in heat in the complete cycle is 1200 J. Find the work done in the BC section.

In a process, the pressure-volume relationship of one mole of an ideal gas is represented by the first quadrant part of the graph 15P + V² - 75 = 0. Find the maximum temperature in the process. [where P and V are expressed in atm and L units respectively]
200 mol of O₂ gas undergoes an isothermal expansion in step AB and a sudden compression in step CA. If the temperature at point B is 600 K, find the work done in the ABCA cycle.
If the specific heat of air at constant volume is 170 cal kg⁻¹K⁻¹, γ = 1.4, and the density of air at STP is 1.29 kgm⁻³, find the mechanical equivalent of heat. [R = 8.314 Jmol⁻¹K⁻¹]

An unknown gas is kept in a cylinder with a piston at one end, having a radius of 5 cm, at 1 atm pressure. If the temperature of the gas is suddenly increased from -10°C to 70°C, the height of the piston decreases by 48.518%. If the initial height of the piston was 35 cm, what is the work done?

The temperature of the cold reservoir of a heat engine is T₂ = 300 K and the temperature of the hot reservoir is T₁. The engine absorbs 200 J of heat from the hot reservoir and rejects 120 J of heat to the cold reservoir. Find the minimum temperature of the hot reservoir.
The figure shows the change in entropy for a change in temperature of a 364 g object. Find the specific heat of the object.
One mole of an ideal gas follows the pressure-volume relationship P = P₀[1 - (V₀/V)²]. Find the change in temperature if its volume changes from V₀ to 2V₀. [P₀ and V₀ are constants.]
150 mol of N₂ gas is taken through the cycle ABCDA. Find the total work done in the cycle.

An ideal gas is heated at constant pressure. If 140 J of heat is supplied, the internal energy increases by 100 J and the temperature increases by 5K. Determine the nature of the gas (mono/di/polyatomic) and the number of moles of gas used. [R = 8 JK⁻¹mol⁻¹]

In an adiabatic process, the temperature of a gas kept at 41 kPa pressure and 28 L volume is increased from 1.3°C to 83.7°C. This increases the pressure of the gas by 151%. What is the work done by the gas?

Three Carnot engines are connected in series, where the sink of one acts as the source for the next. Here, T₁ > T₂ > T₃ > T₄. If the efficiencies of the three engines are equal, find the relationship between T₁, T₂, T₃, and T₄.

2 mol of an ideal diatomic gas is taken through a three-step cycle. Step-01: In an isochoric process, the temperature is raised from 300 K to 800 K. Step-02: Through an isothermal expansion, it returns to a pressure equal to the initial pressure. Step-03: Through an isobaric compression, the gas returns to its initial state, completing the cycle. Find the change in entropy in each step.

In a process, the pressure-volume relationship of one mole of an ideal gas is represented by the first quadrant part of the graph P + V³ - 32 = 0. Find the maximum temperature in the process. [where P and V are expressed in atm and L units respectively]
100 mol of He gas is expanded along the path AB. This path follows the relationship P ∝ V². Find the change in entropy along the path AB.

A mixture contains 2 mol of helium and 5 mol of hydrogen. Find the value of γ for the mixture and the ratio of the heat required to raise the temperature by the same amount in isochoric and isobaric processes.

0.83 L of gas at 1.8 atm pressure is expanded adiabatically, increasing the volume by 35.8%. If the same amount of work is done in an isothermal process at the initial temperature, by what percentage will the volume increase? [γ = 5/3]

The temperature of the cold reservoir of a heat engine is T₂ = 600 K and the temperature of the hot reservoir is T₁. The engine absorbs 2000 J of heat from the hot reservoir and rejects 1200 J of heat to the cold reservoir. Find the minimum temperature of the hot reservoir.
options: প্রতিটি ধাপে তাপশক্তির পরিবর্তন নির্ণয় কর।
BC প্রক্রিয়ায় অন্তঃস্থ শক্তির পরিবর্তন কত?

questions.question: If the gas used is N₂ and the number of moles is 4 mol- (a) Find the change in heat energy in each step. (b) What is the work done in path AB? (c) What is the change in internal energy in process BC? **Set-D** 07. 1 mol H₂ গ্যাসকে 5.2 cm² প্রস্থচ্ছেদের ক্ষেত্রফলবিশিষ্ট পিস্টনযুক্ত সিলিন্ডারে 1.2 atm চাপে রাখা আছে। 50 J তাপ দেয়া হলে পিস্টনটি 22.6 cm উপরে উঠে। যদি চাপ স্থির থাকে তবে গ্যাসের তাপমাত্রা কত বৃদ্ধি পায়? [1 mol of H₂ gas is kept in a cylinder with a piston of cross-sectional area 5.2 cm² at a pressure of 1.2 atm. If 50 J of heat is supplied, the piston rises by 22.6 cm. If the pressure remains constant, what is the increase in the temperature of the gas?
A piece of ice falls from a height h to the ground, and 40% of its potential energy is converted into heat, causing 1% of the ice block to melt. h =?
If a refrigerator absorbs 300 J of heat from its inner chamber and releases 400 J of heat to the environment, then C.O.P =?
When 500 J of work is done on a system, the system rejects 300 J of heat. What is the change in the internal energy of the system?
If a refrigerator does 105 J of work to remove 350 J of heat from its inner chamber, how much heat does it reject to the environment? [The size of the refrigerator is 6 x 3 x 2 m³]
If a Carnot engine operating between 40°C and 50°C absorbs 329 J of heat from the reservoir per cycle, what is its efficiency?

Two glass bulbs of equal volume, connected by a narrow tube of negligible volume, are filled with gas at 0°C and 76cm pressure. One bulb is placed in melting ice and the other in water at 62°C. Find the new pressure inside the bulb at 62°C.
One mole of an ideal gas undergoes a process that follows the equation P = P₀(1 + (2V₀/V)⁻¹), where P₀ and V₀ are constants. Find the change in temperature when the volume changes from V₀ to 2V₀.
If the linear kinetic energy of a gas molecule is 6.27 × 10⁻²¹ J and its total kinetic energy is 10.45 × 10⁻²¹ J, find the number of atoms in the gas molecule.

When a person inhales, their lungs can hold 5.5 Litres of air at a body temperature of 98.4°F and 1 atmospheric pressure. If the amount of oxygen in this air is 20.65%, how many oxygen molecules can the lungs hold?
A bucket of water is placed in a 50 m³ room at a temperature of 15°C, and the initial relative humidity is 40%. What amount of water will evaporate? [Saturated vapor pressure at 15°C is 1.6kPa]

At sea level at 45° latitude and 0°C temperature, 1 nitrogen molecule is in motion and is allowed to rise freely upwards. The molecule rises up to h₁ m and stops. Find the value of h₁. [Mass of the Earth is 6 × 10²⁴ kg and radius is 6400 km]
A Carnot engine has a sink at 27°C and its efficiency is 50%. To make its efficiency 60%, by how much should the temperature of its source be increased?
Using an electric kettle, the temperature of 2 kg of water is raised from 25°C to 80°C. Calculate the change in entropy.

Three different liquids A, B, and C of equal mass have temperatures of 12°C, 19°C, and 28°C respectively. If A is mixed with B, the final temperature is 16°C. If B is mixed with C, the final temperature is 23°C. What will be the temperature if A is mixed with C?

A reversible engine converts 1/6th of the heat into work. When the temperature of the source is kept constant and the temperature of the sink is decreased by 62°C, the efficiency of the engine doubles. Find the temperature of the sink.

An ideal gas at an initial temperature T₁ has an initial volume of 2 m³. After adiabatic expansion, its volume becomes 4 m³. Then, it is expanded isothermally to a volume of 10 m³. In the next step, it is compressed adiabatically until its temperature returns to T₁. What is its final volume?

A cylinder contains 0.001 m³ of gas at 300 K and 10⁵ Pa pressure. The gas is first expanded isothermally and then again expanded adiabatically, with the expansion ratio being 1:2 in each case. Calculate the total amount of work done. (Consider γ = 1.4).

An iron nail of mass 10 g is heated in a burner flame for some time. The heated nail is then immersed in 100 g of water at 10°C. This causes the water temperature to rise to 20°C. Find the temperature of the nail before it was immersed in the water. [Specific heat of iron = 0.11 kcal/kg°C]

A gas has a volume of 4.3 L at 1.2 atm pressure and 310 K temperature. The gas is compressed adiabatically to a volume of 0.76 L. Find the (i) final pressure and (ii) final temperature of the gas. [Consider the gas as an ideal gas with γ = 1.4]

A nuclear bomb explosion creates a fireball of 100 m radius and 10⁵ K temperature. If the fireball expands adiabatically to a 1000 m radius, what will be its probable temperature? (Ratio of specific heats, γ = 1.66)

A Carnot engine's source and sink temperatures are 500 K and 375 K, respectively. If the engine absorbs 252 × 10⁴ J of heat per cycle, find: (i) The efficiency of the engine, (ii) The amount of work done per cycle, and (iii) The amount of heat rejected per cycle.
A Carnot cycle initially operates at 327°C. If the compression or expansion ratio in each step is 1:6, find the minimum temperature and efficiency of the Carnot cycle. [γ = 1.4]

The temperature of a patient's body is measured as 45°C with a faulty thermometer. If the ice point and steam point on this thermometer are marked as 3°C and 107°C respectively, find the actual temperature of the patient's body on the Fahrenheit scale.
A motor tire is pumped to a pressure of 2 atmospheres at 15°C and it suddenly bursts. By how much will its temperature fall? [γ = 1.4]

At normal temperature and pressure, the volume of one kilomole of helium is 22.42 L. If the specific heat of helium at constant volume is 3.0 cal mol⁻¹K⁻¹, find its specific heat at constant pressure. (Given, density of mercury = 13.6 × 10³ kgm⁻³; J = 4200 J/kcal)

An ideal Carnot engine has source and sink temperatures of 450 K and 350 K. If the engine takes 1 kcal of heat from the source per cycle, then find: (i) The heat rejected to the sink per cycle, (ii) The efficiency of the engine, and (iii) The amount of work done per cycle. [J = 4.184 kJ/kcal]

At a temperature of 40°C and 76 cm of Hg pressure, a certain amount of air is adiabatically expanded to double its volume. What will be the temperature? If the temperature is then increased by 20°C from this new temperature, what will be its pressure? [γ = 1.4]
A Carnot engine converts 1/4th of the input heat into work. If the temperature of its sink is further reduced by 70°C, its efficiency doubles. Find the temperatures of the source and the sink.
Air at 100°C is adiabatically compressed to half its volume. Find the change in temperature.
Define "heat and temperature".
What is an isothermal process? The temperature of a certain mass of gas is 40°C. If its volume is doubled through an adiabatic process, what will be the final temperature? (γ = 1.4)

A cylinder contains 10 litres of air at 3 atm pressure and 300 K temperature. (i) If the pressure is suddenly doubled, what will be the volume and temperature of the air? (ii) If the pressure is doubled very slowly, what will be the volume and temperature?
An amount of dry gas at standard temperature and pressure is compressed to one-third of its volume (i) isothermally and (ii) adiabatically. Find the final pressure in each case. (γ = 1.4)

The height of the Eiffel Tower in France is 336 meters. The maximum temperature in summer is 100°F and the minimum temperature in winter is 0°F. How much taller is the tower in summer than in winter? The tower is made of iron and the coefficient of linear expansion of iron is α = 12 × 10⁻⁶ °C⁻¹.
An inventor claims that his engine, operating between 700 K and 400 K, has an efficiency of 48%. Is his claim correct?

Find the molar specific heat at constant volume (Cv) and at constant pressure (Cp) for oxygen. The density of oxygen at normal temperature and pressure is 1.43 kgm⁻³, normal pressure = 1.01 × 10⁵ Nm⁻², and γ = 1.4.

A lead bullet of mass m is fired at a tree. The velocity of the bullet upon entering and exiting the tree is 500 ms⁻¹ and 300 ms⁻¹ respectively. Assuming 50% of the lost energy is deposited as heat in the bullet, find the increase in the bullet's temperature. [Specific heat of lead = 0.031 kcal kg⁻¹°C⁻¹; 1 kcal kg⁻¹°C⁻¹ = 4.184 kJkg⁻¹°C⁻¹]
The volume of a gas at 15°C is suddenly increased 4 times. What is its final temperature? (Given γ = 5/3)
What is the coefficient of performance for a refrigerator that rejects 700 J of heat to the hot reservoir and removes 400 J from the cold reservoir?
A system absorbs 900 J of heat and does 300 J of work. What is the change in its internal energy?
What is the maximum possible efficiency of an engine operating between 127°C and 427°C?
In an adiabatic process with γ = 1.4, if the pressure of a diatomic gas is increased by 0.5%, the volume of the gas will decrease by?
The work done when the temperature of 1 mole of an ideal gas is increased by 1 K at normal temperature and pressure is?
When 10 g of water is heated from 0°C to 40°C, the change in entropy will be?

A gas chamber at 29°C contains CO₂ gas with a density of 100 kgm⁻³ at 1 atm pressure. The chamber bursts if the gas pressure is increased to 2 atm. What is the change in the density of the gas at the chamber's final temperature? (γ = 1.33)

Mr. Rafiq dropped a 225 gm block of ice at 0°C from a certain height. Due to the law of conservation of energy upon hitting the ground, 10% of the ice block melted. If this ice block is to be converted into steam at 100°C, what will be the change in entropy?
A Carnot engine has an efficiency of 50% when its sink is at 27°C. To increase the efficiency to 60%, by how much should the source temperature be raised?
Calculate the change in entropy when 10 gm of ice at -10°C is converted to water at 0°C.
What is the change in entropy when 5 kg of ice at 0°C is converted into water at 0°C?

Ahnaf dropped a 250 gm block of ice at 0°C from a certain height. Due to the heat generated from the principle of conservation of energy upon impact, 10% of the ice block melted. From what height did Ahnaf drop the ice block?
A Carnot engine operates between 27°C and 180°C. The engine takes 8.6 × 10⁴ J of heat from the source. What is the amount of work done by the engine?
A Carnot engine has a source and sink at 229°C and 106°C respectively. If the engine absorbs 600 × 10⁵ cal/cycle of heat, find the heat rejected per cycle.
A waterfall is 900 meters high. If it is assumed that half of the kinetic energy of the falling water is converted into heat, what will be the rise in temperature
What is the difference in entropy between 1 gm of water at 100°C and 1 gm of steam at 100°C at 1 atmospheric pressure? [Latent heat of vaporization at 100°C = 540 cal/gm]
What is the efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between the steam point and the ice point?
At what temperature do the Fahrenheit and Kelvin scales give the same reading?

A constant volume hydrogen thermometer, when placed in liquid air, ice, and steam, indicates pressures of 23.3 cm, 75.1 cm, and 102.5 cm of mercury respectively. What is the temperature of the liquid air?
In an adiabatic process, the volume of air is increased to double. If the initial pressure was one atmosphere, what will be the final pressure?
The PDF `f(t)` is the rate of change of unreliability, `F(t) = 1 - R(t)`.
The hazard rate `z(t)` is defined as the conditional probability of failing in the interval `[t, t+dt]` given survival up to `t`, divided by the interval length `dt`.
Using the formula for conditional probability `P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B) / P(B)`:
Since `P(t ≤ T < t+dt) ≈ f(t) dt`, we can substitute this in:
